items.acceptanceCriteria: Equipment documentation is available
Previous inspection reports are checked
Daily pre-operational inspection checklists are checked (if applicable)
Preventive Maintenance is established and updated records are maintained
Operator is certified(for the inspected crane type/model) and licensed
Load rating chart is applicable to the configured boom and is legible
An operating manual is available (English)
A sign is posted warning the operator not to rely solely on any automatic device as a substitute for safe operating practice
Crane is painted safety yellow
Crane is painted safety yellow-and-black stripes on offshore facilities
Rated capacity of crane is marked(Arabic & English)
A fire extinguisher with minimum rating of 10 BC is installed in the cab or at the machinery housing
Electrical apparatus is working correctly without excessive dirt, deterioration or moisture accumulation
Control levers are all operable from the operator station
Control pedals are functioning correctly
All controls are labeled as to their functions and within reach of operator
Guards are fitted and secured to cover exposed moving parts
Lubrication chart is available on the side boom
All moving parts that require lubrication are lubricated and accessible
Tires and wheels are in condition and as recommended inflation pressure
Boom angle indicator is functioning correctly
Boom hoist shut off is functioning correctly
Load indicator is functioning correctly
Capacity indicator is functioning correctly
Gasoline, diesel, electric, or other power plants to be checked for improper performance or noncompliance with other applicable standards
Emergency shut down is operating satisfactorily
Engine throttle is functioning satisfactorily
Battery is secure and ventilated
Engine and Fuel level gauges are functioning correctly
Exhaust system is not corroded, and is guarded or insulated where necessary to prevent personal contact with hot surfaces
Engine oil level is adequate.
Hydraulic/Pneumatic systems are not leaking
Oil and fuel tanks leaks are checked
Hydraulic/Pneumatic systems are not leaking (pumps, valves, lines, etc.).
Hook assembly labeling and manufacturer data is clearly marked
Hook's weight is clearly marked/printed on the hook
Safe working load of hook is clearly marked on the item
Hook does not show defects such as nicks, cracks and gouges
Hook is not bent or twisted
Hook is not distorted in the throat opening

• Max. allowable throat opening is 5% not to exceed 1/4 in. (6 mm) , or as per manufacturer recommendations Maximum wear in the hook bowl is not exceeding 10%(compared to new hook) or as per manufacturer recommendations
Hook is not cracked, gouged or shows nicks
Hook can lock(if it is a self-locking hook)
Hook latch is operative
All critical parts are not cracked, broken, bent, corroded welds or excessively worn.
worn, cracked, or distorted parts such as pins, bearings, shafts, gears, rollers, and locking and clamping devices are checked
Boom bushings and pins are not worn and are secure
Machinery shall be stationary while lubricants are being applied and protection provided

When using boom hoist reeving or hydraulic cylinder system with maximum load capacity suspended, the boom hoist mechanism shall be capable of raising or lowering the boom, stopping boom motion, and holding
Sheave grooves are free from surface defects and lubricated
Rope Carrying sheaves have close-fittings guards or other suitable devices
Lower block sheaves have close-fittings guards
All moving parts of the side boom tractor for which lubrication is specified, including rope and chain, should be regularly lubricated
Bearings and bushings are checked and lubricated
Boom stops are fitted and undamaged
Bearings and bushings are undamaged, secure and lubricated
Load hoist brake function is satisfactory
Load lifting and lowering operations are satisfactory
Two full wraps, at least, of rope remain on the drum when the hook is in its extreme low working position
Rope ends are anchored to the drum by clamps or as per manufacturer recommendations
Drums are provided with a guidance or other means to prevent rope from jumping off the drum
Power controlled lowering is operational and meets the speed and load criteria as per manufacturers specifications
Positive braking means is available, controllable by the operator, to prevent drum rotation in the lowering direction
Broken wires— in running ropes, six randomly

distributed broken wires in six rope diameters or three broken wires in one strand in six rope diameters. Broken wires— in Standing ropes (guys), including end connections, six randomly distributed broken wires in six rope diameters or three broken wires in one strand in six rope diameters.
Removal criteria for rope replacement , Reduction from nominal diameter of more than 5%.
The rope does not have kinking, crushing, birdcaging, evidence of heat damage, ubstranding, core corrosion, main strand displacement or any other damages
Boom hoist mechanism (raising and lowering) is properly operating
Boom hoist brake and clutch are correctly operating
Boom hoist brake and clutch have adjustments to compensate for wear
Locking pawl and ratchet is in good working condition to prevent inadvertent lowering of the boom
Two full wraps, at least, of rope remain on the drum when the hook is in its extreme low working position
Rope ends are anchored to the drum by clamps or as per manufacturer recommendations
Drums are provided with a guidance or other means to prevent rope from jumping off the drum
Broken wires— in running ropes, six randomly distributed broken wires in six rope diameters or three broken wires in one strand in six rope
diameters Broken wires—(guys), including end connections, six randomly distributed broken wires in six rope diameters or three broken wires in one strand in six rope diameters
Removal criteria for rope replacement, Reduction from nominal diameter of more than 5%
The rope does not have kinking, crushing, birdcaging, evidence of heat
damage, ubstranding, core corrosion, main strand displacement or any other damages Boom hoist cylinder and associated hydraulic system is operating correctly
Boom hoist cylinder seals are not leaking
There is no worn, cracked, or distorted parts such as pins, bearings, shafts, gears, rollers, and locking and clamping devices
Track rollers and followers are turning and not loose, worn or seized
Sheaves and drums are not cracked or worn
The slope (level) indicator is functional to indicate the extent that the side boom tractor chassis is

out-of-level and accuracy of slope indication is within deg of actual slope. When lifting on slopes or unusual ground conditions or where external restraints are utilized for stability, the appropriate rated load capacity has been determined and verified
Drive chain is not stretched excessively
Drive gearbox operates without excessive noise or leakage
Chain drive sprockets are not worn
Loose bolts or rivets are checked and secure
Means for fastening stabilizer floats to stabilizers when in use are provided.
Propelling forward is accepted
Propelling backward is accepted
Transmission gearbox functioning well and gears are operational
Steering to the right is operational
Steering to the left is operational
Travel locks are operational and remain in engagement
All safety limit devices are operational
Cab glass is made of safety glazing material
Visibility on the boom side of the cab such that the uppermost point of the boom is visible at all times.
Road mirrors are fitted and undamaged
Fire extinguisher is available in the cab (minimum rating is 10BC)
Gauges and instrumentations are in good working condition
Brakes are in working condition
Parking brake is in working condition
Clutch is in good working condition
Gear selector is properly working
Headlights are working
Brake lights are working
Turn signals are working
Ignition system is operating correctly
Throttle is operating correctly
Battery is secure and ventilated
Gauges are in good working condition
Engine exhaust system is guarded or insulated where necessary to prevent personal contact with hot surfaces
Engine exhaust gases are piped to the outside of the cab and discharged in a direction away from the operator
Oil level is sufficient
Torque converter is in good working condition
Main transmission is in good working condition
Auxiliary transmission is in good working condition
Tires and rims are undamaged and serviceable
Means to hold all stabilizers in the retracted position while traveling and in the extended position is provided.
Outrigger pads are securely fitted and undamaged
Pad pins are locked and undamaged
Extension cylinders are working correctly and undamaged
Vertical cylinders are working correctly and undamaged
Tires and rims are undamaged and serviceable
Means to hold all stabilizers in the retracted position while traveling and in the extended position is provided.
Outrigger pads are securely fitted and undamaged
Pad pins are locked and undamaged
Extension cylinders are working correctly and undamaged
Vertical cylinders are working correctly and undamaged
Boom tip section is undamaged, has no cracks or distortion
boom extension cylinder is properly working with no signs of leaks
Guide sheaves are not worn and are well lubricated
Sheave grooves are free from surface defects
Bushings/bearings are not worn and are well lubricated
Guards are fitted and secured to cover exposed moving parts
Rope end is anchored and rope clamp is tightened in accordance with the manufacturers recommendations
Counterweights is used in accordance with the manufacturer’s specifications
Hydraulic hoses have no leaks
Complete counterweight and arm assembly shows no cracks or deformation
